answer:
**Yama Smṛti, Chapter 2.**

This Sanskrit Smṛti text (a genre of penitential and legal literature) prescribes the described punishments in two immediately adjacent verses:

> With his body smeared with ashes and fumigated with the hair and bones of a corpse, / the king, having confiscated all his property, should banish from the kingdom the one who has abandoned his sacred fire. // YSS_2.24 //[^1]

> Being fanned with the hair of a corpse, his head adorned with a garland of it, / the king should make the man who violates convention go naked. // YSS_2.25 //[^2]

Earlier verses in the same chapter specify that the offender in the first case is a Brahmin.[^3]

[^1]: Yama Smṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/#p-c0845b).
[^2]: Yama Smṛti, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/#p-05b685).
[^3]: Yama Smṛti, Chapter 2, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/#p-c4ccca).

args.query: hair and bones of a corpse
